conditional_states.condition: Cards can be multi-selected for bulk move or edit.
Columns paginate or lazy-load cards beyond an initial window.
Board is shared/real-time and another user may move a card.
A drag is committed against a column another user already changed (optimistic move rejected).
Moves must queue and reconcile when connectivity returns.
User may view a column but cannot move cards into or out of it.
Drag works via long-press on coarse-pointer devices with a tap-to-move fallback.
Card move/reflow animation must degrade to an instant position change.

rationale: A kanban board is a drag-driven, often collaborative surface, so the active (dragging) state, per-column empty state, and the loading/error of the board fetch are all required. Drag-and-drop must have a keyboard equivalent (pick up, move, drop) and an optimistic move must reconcile on conflict so a rejected drag does not leave a card in the wrong column.
evidence: WCAG 2.2 2.1.1 (Keyboard) and 2.5.7 (Dragging Movements): drag operations need a single-pointer / keyboard alternative.
WAI-ARIA: announce drag pickup, target column, and drop result via status messages (4.1.3).
Frappe Helpdesk / CRM and Trello-style optimistic-move patterns: reconcile and revert card position on server rejection.
